---
title: "Group"
date: "2026-06-25"
categories: 
  - Math > Abstract Algebra > Group Theory
---

# 1. Group
## 1.1. Definition
**(i)** A ***group*** $\langle G, \ast \rangle$ is a binary algebraic structure which satisfies the following conditions:

- $(a \ast b) \ast c = a \ast (b \ast c), \forall a, b, c \in G$.

- $\exists e \in G$ such that $e \ast a = a \ast e = a, \forall a \in G$.

- $\forall a \in G$, $\exists b \in G$ such that $a \ast b = e = b \ast a$.

**(ii)** A group $\langle G, \ast \rangle$ is called an ***abelian group*** if $\ast$ is commutative.

## 1.2. Example
**(i)** $\langle \mathbb{Z}, + \rangle, \langle \mathbb{Q}, + \rangle$, and $\langle \mathbb{R}, + \rangle$ are abelian groups.

**(ii)** $\langle \mathbb{Q}^{\ast}, \cdot \rangle$ and $\langle \mathbb{R}^{\ast}, \cdot \rangle$ are abelian groups.

**(iii)** $\langle \mathbb{R}^{m \times n}, + \rangle$ is an abelian group.

**(iv)** $\langle GL(n, \mathbb{R}), \cdot \rangle$ and $\langle SL(n, \mathbb{R}), \cdot \rangle$ are not abelian groups.
